rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT lAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, OCTOBER 17, 2014, LINE 1. CROSS DEFENDANT VLADIMIR ABRAMOV Motion To Compel Arbitration DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to present sufficient evidence for the Court to make a determination per CCP 1281.2. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Plaintiffs Gabriela Geyer, Travis Geyer'S Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 17, 2014, line 2. Hearing Of Plaintiffs Gabriela Geyer and Travis Geyer'S Motion For Sanctions continued to November 20, 2014 to be heard by the Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 17, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend as to individual and class action based on violation of CCP 1940.8 and B&P 8358. Complaint admits that CCP 1940.8 has been complied with. Moving defendant does not owe any duty to plaintiff(s) pursuant to B&P 8358. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to individual action based on failures alleged in paragraph 17 of SAC to allege fraud-based violation of B&P 17200 with requisite specificity. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 17, 2014, line 5. PLAINTIFFS LARRY BROWN, CHRISTOPHER ASHE, LUTRECE DAVIS, IRMATINE BOLDS, PAUL GALINDO's Motion To Sever And Order Separate Trial On Causes Of Action 1-9, 11 And 12 From The Cross Complaint Of Frank Meyers And Place Of Franks, Llc And To Sever And Order Separate Trial On The Cross-Complaints Of Rickesh And Ashok Patel Plaintiff's Motion to Sever and Order Separate Trial is GRANTED as follows: Complaint and the 10th cause of action for indemnity of cross-complaint Myers v. Fried is severed for purposes of trial from the remaining cross-complaints. No opposition filed. = (501/GLW)
